medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ y/o Male with history of CAD s/p BMS-->LAD in ___, history of duodenal ulcer in ___. He presented to the hospital with left shoulder pain, found to have ECG changes suggestive of inferolateral ischemia, and positive cardiac biomarkers. Over last 7 days, he developed the shoulder pain and had difficulty sleeping the last 2 days, which was worse last night. He reported no exertional component, shortness of breath, or frank chest pain.
medical_history.past_medical_history: CAD s/p proximal LAD stent in ___. Moderate to severe mitral regurgitation, aortic insufficiency, peripheral vascular disease, stones, basal cell carcinoma of left ear, glaucoma.
medical_history.family_history: No gastric cancer, colon cancer, diabetes mellitus, or coronary artery disease.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: Temp 97.8, BP 131/68, HR 62, RR 18, Sat 97% RA. General: Elderly thin male in no acute distress, oriented x3.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Grossly appropriate, alert and oriented x3. Cardiovascular: PMI located in ___ intercostal space, midclavicular line, normal S1, S2, no rubs, murmurs at apex, no thrills or lifts. Respiratory: Chest clear to auscultation bilaterally, no crackles, wheezes, or rhonchi. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, no hepatosplenomegaly or tenderness. Extremities: No cyanosis, clubbing, or edema. Pulses: Carotid, femoral, popliteal, and dorsalis pedis 2+ bilaterally.
